qa_pairs.question: How can I access the job monitoring interface in Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: You can access the job monitoring interface by navigating to the 'Jobs' tab and selecting 'Active Jobs'.

qa_pairs.question: How do I filter jobs in the Open OnDemand job monitoring interface?
qa_pairs.answer: You can filter jobs by using the 'Filter' text box located at the top right of the interface.

qa_pairs.question: How can I sort the jobs displayed in the Open OnDemand monitoring interface?
qa_pairs.answer: You can sort jobs by clicking on the column headers, for example, to sort by job status (running, completed, failed, etc.).

qa_pairs.question: What should I click to see more details about a specific job in the queue?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on the '>' symbol to the left of a job will show you more details about it.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of detailed information is available when I click on the '>' symbol next to a job?
qa_pairs.answer: When you click on the '>' symbol, you will see details such as the job's start/end time, node list, and the account charged.

qa_pairs.question: How can I view all jobs in the queue, not just active ones?
qa_pairs.answer: To show all jobs in the queue, click on the drop-down menu at the top right and select 'All Jobs'.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a more detailed view of my jobs beyond the Open OnDemand interface?
qa_pairs.answer: A more detailed view of your jobs can still be found using the my.SciNet portal.
